title: Tokyo 3 Repairs
wikitext:
[]: Tokyo-3 Repairs             Quote Post by Zeak » Mon Dec 15, 2008 7:47 am Tokyo 3 gets one hell of a beating over the series. There are mountains leveled. City blocks sat on. Roads well there are not anymore. I guess the point I am trying to make here is Tokyo 3 must be so well adapted to attacks that it can repair itself quite quickly. I guess this also brings Rebuild into account here. Ill use the fight against Ramial for example a whole city block gets blown below the surface. I would like to hear what people thoughts are on just how Tokyo 3 works (I do understand that anami laws of physics come into play). So what do people think? Is Tokyo 3 able to repair itself in record time? If you need me to reword it I can

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Dec 15, 2008 8:46 am So, are you basically asking to discuss if Tokyo-3 is better adapted at repairng itself quicker?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Dec 15, 2008 9:05 am "Repair itself"? Is Tokyo-3 alive or something? "Anami"... Oh, Zeak.

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Mon Dec 15, 2008 9:08 am :P Thr people who live there. It looks as if they can rebuild the city faster than they can remove Ramiel. The ammount of distrycuion caused is insane but you dont see any of it untill after Rei II dies

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Dec 15, 2008 11:14 am I'd say they probably didn't fix that block. You just can't see it because the hole is hidden behind the other blocks whenever they're up. At best they just slapped on a patch in the armor layers and quickly paved that over.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Dec 15, 2008 3:02 pm Ever since the repeated episodes of Giant Monster invasion starting with the many Godzilla incidents in the 1950s, Japanese cities have been engineered to provide almost overnight repair facilities. Sheesh, didn't you know that?

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Mon Dec 15, 2008 3:35 pm 1. An army of construction workers. 2. An Eva that can repair things with it's AT field. 3. Self repairing buildings. 4. Anno using the god-like powers of creativity. Pick your favorite explanation.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Mon Dec 15, 2008 3:41 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Japanese cities have been engineered to provide almost overnight repair facilities.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Dec 15, 2008 3:49 pm 2. An Eva that can repair things with it's AT field.

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Mon Dec 15, 2008 4:04 pm I didn't mean it literally. But could a skilled Eva pilot manipulate it to push things into place or out of the way, thus making it easier to rebuild?

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Mon Dec 15, 2008 6:08 pm Introducing a new shoot-off of Webuild: "Rebuild of Tokyo-3: It Can (Not) Be Fixed"

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Mon Dec 15, 2008 6:10 pm They designed and built an armoured fortress city in the time it normally takes Dublin City council to build one tunnel. throwing enough manpower and cash at a problem such as Angel damage would sure as hell solve it quickly enough. And who's to say they didnt use some form of modular construction? Building the city out of what would essentially be prefabricated parts would certainly speed things up. Concrete Lego Tokyo-3 anyone?

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Mon Dec 15, 2008 6:14 pm Nanobot construction workers. A floating gas of flying ants will always work faster and more efficiently than a group of construction workers who demand for lunch and potty breaks.

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Tue Dec 16, 2008 1:43 am Dartz wrote: And who's to say they didn’t use some form of modular construction? Building the city out of what would essentially be prefabricated parts would certainly speed things up. Concrete Lego Tokyo-3 anyone?

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Tue Dec 16, 2008 5:44 am [URL=http://imageshack.us] [/URL] [URL=http://g.imageshack.us/img101/362497bobthebuilderpostxg3.jpg/1/] [/URL] 'nuff said.

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Tue Dec 16, 2008 6:45 am To be honest I doubt it would work.

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Tue Dec 16, 2008 3:11 pm Action_Bastard and master_lloyd have both made my day, would you each like an internet, they're all the rage these days! =D

[]:             Quote Post by MatrixRefugee » Tue Dec 16, 2008 4:51 pm TheAyanamiOtaku wrote: Nanobot construction workers. A floating gas of flying ants will always work faster and more efficiently than a group of construction workers who demand for lunch and potty breaks.

[]:             Quote Post by jim_loves_randy » Wed Dec 17, 2008 12:51 am Tokyo-3 probably has a massive budget. Also, being in the future where science and technology is a magic helps.

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Wed Dec 17, 2008 1:38 am I guess with enought money you can do anything. Looking at a house these days getting built. About a week after the slab is down the walls are up.
